intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer human, imperfect, intimate presentation over polished digital perfection
facade.label: Handwritten recipe cards
facade.acceptImplies: The app should feel personally curated, warm, and approachable—like a friend's notebook rather than a cooking encyclopedia
facade.rejectImplies: Users want clean, modern, professional presentation—trust comes from polish and clarity, not warmth

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer human, imperfect, intimate presentation over polished digital perfection
evidence.implication: The app should feel personally curated, warm, and approachable—like a friend's notebook rather than a cooking encyclopedia

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer clean, uncluttered interfaces that feel approachable rather than overwhelming
facade.axisTargeted: Visual complexity vs. simplicity; light vs. dark palette
facade.label: Bright and minimal
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward spacious layouts, high contrast, white/light backgrounds, simple iconography, and reduced visual noise
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer richer color, darker tones, denser information density, or more decorative elements

facade.hypothesis: Users want zero-friction recipe selection — one tap starts cooking, no scrolling or reading
facade.axisTargeted: gesture vs click; instant vs animated transitions
facade.label: Instant tap-to-cook
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and directness over discovery; wants friction-free access to 'just tell me what to make'
facade.rejectImplies: User wants to browse, explore, or see options before committing; values choice and deliberation in recipe selection

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer calorie/prep-time metrics over narrative descriptions
facade.label: Just the numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Recipe app should lead with quantifiable facts (servings, time, calories) before any story or flavor description
facade.rejectImplies: Users need context and story even when time-constrained; pure metrics feel cold and unhelpful

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking avoid apps feeling cluttered or overwhelming; generous spacing and touch-friendly buttons reduce friction and cognitive load
facade.label: Big buttons, lots of whitespace
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize comfort and ease-of-use; embrace air in the layout; make every action low-effort and forgiving
facade.rejectImplies: Users tolerate or prefer compact, information-dense layouts; spacing is less of a blocker than clarity or speed; test tighter card designs next

facade.hypothesis: A voice that's witty and self-aware—gently mocking the cooking process itself—will resonate with people frustrated by recipes
facade.label: Sarcastic sidekick tone
facade.acceptImplies: User wants personality and humor baked into the app. Expects the app to validate their frustration, not lecture them. Could support casual, conversational language throughout.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers straightforward, no-nonsense guidance. Humor feels gimmicky or patronizing. Axis leans toward clarity and efficiency over entertainment.

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants a calm, meditative cooking experience vs. fast-paced, time-saving urgency and efficiency focus
Whether user needs heavy filtering (dietary, skill level, time) vs. simple discovery with minimal decision friction
axes.poleA: Lots of filters or keep it simple

axes.label: Whether user wants satisfying progress feedback and celebration vs. invisible, frictionless interactions that get out of the way

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, meditative cooking experience vs. fast-paced, time-saving urgency and efficiency focus
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user needs heavy filtering (dietary, skill level, time) vs. simple discovery with minimal decision friction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants satisfying progress feedback and celebration vs. invisible, frictionless interactions that get out of the way
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking are intimidated by perfectionism and respond better to real, imperfect, slightly chaotic food imagery than to styled food photography
facade.axisTargeted: organic vs digital / intimate vs expansive
facade.label: Messy ingredient photos
facade.acceptImplies: The app should embrace casual, unstaged visuals—crumpled recipe notes, half-prepped ingredients, real kitchen chaos—to lower the barrier to entry and build confidence in non-cooks
facade.rejectImplies: Users who hate cooking still want visual clarity and reassurance; they may need polished, well-lit photography to trust the recipe and feel capable of executing it

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers only the fastest, easiest meals with minimal ingredients and steps. Perfect for busy people who want food without the fuss.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Meals in 15 minutes or less

Garlic Butter Pasta

8 min4 ingredients

Boil pasta, toss with butter, garlic, and salt. Done.

Avocado Toast

5 min3 ingredients

Toast bread, mash avocado, spread. Add salt and pepper.

Egg Fried Rice

12 min5 ingredients

Scramble eggs, add rice, soy sauce, and frozen veg. Stir.

Save Your Favorites

Bookmark recipes you actually want to make
